verses.latin_vulgate: Ab Omnipotente non sunt abscondita tempora :  qui autem noverunt eum,  ignorant dies illius.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַדּ֗וּעַ מִ֭שַּׁדַּי לֹא־נִצְפְּנ֣וּ עִתִּ֑ים וְ֝יֹדְעָ֗ו לֹא־חָ֥זוּ יָמָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: Why, seeing times are not hidden from the Almighty, do they that know him not see his days?

verses.latin_vulgate: Alii terminos transtulerunt ;  diripuerunt greges, et paverunt eos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גְּבֻל֥וֹת יַשִּׂ֑יגוּ עֵ֥דֶר גָּ֝זְל֗וּ וַיִּרְעֽוּ׃
verses.king_james: Some remove the landmarks; they violently take away flocks, and feed thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Asinum pupillorum abegerunt,  et abstulerunt pro pignore bovem viduæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חֲמ֣וֹר יְתוֹמִ֣ים יִנְהָ֑גוּ יַ֝חְבְּל֗וּ שׁ֣וֹר אַלְמָנָֽה׃
verses.king_james: They drive away the ass of the fatherless, they take the widow’s ox for a pledge.

verses.latin_vulgate: Subverterunt pauperum viam,  et oppresserunt pariter mansuetos terræ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַטּ֣וּ אֶבְיֹנִ֣ים מִדָּ֑רֶךְ יַ֥חַד חֻ֝בְּא֗וּ עֲנִיֵּי־אָֽרֶץ׃
verses.king_james: They turn the needy out of the way: the poor of the earth hide themselves together.

verses.latin_vulgate: Alii quasi onagri in deserto egrediuntur ad opus suum :  vigilantes ad prædam, præparant panem liberis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵ֤ן פְּרָאִ֨ים ׀ בַּ֥מִּדְבָּ֗ר יָצְא֣וּ בְּ֭פׇעֳלָם מְשַׁחֲרֵ֣י לַטָּ֑רֶף עֲרָבָ֥ה ל֥וֹ לֶ֝֗חֶם לַנְּעָרִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Behold, as wild asses in the desert, go they forth to their work; rising betimes for a prey: the wilderness yieldeth food for them and for their children.

verses.latin_vulgate: Agrum non suum demetunt,  et vineam ejus, quem vi oppresserint, vindemiant.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּ֭שָּׂדֶה בְּלִיל֣וֹ (יקצירו) [יִקְצ֑וֹרוּ] וְכֶ֖רֶם רָשָׁ֣ע יְלַקֵּֽשׁוּ׃
verses.king_james: They reap every one his corn in the field: and they gather the vintage of the wicked.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nudos dimittunt homines, indumenta tollentes,  quibus non est operimentum in frigore :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָר֣וֹם יָ֭לִינוּ מִבְּלִ֣י לְב֑וּשׁ וְאֵ֥ין כְּ֝ס֗וּת בַּקָּרָֽה׃
verses.king_james: They cause the naked to lodge without clothing, that they have no covering in the cold.

verses.latin_vulgate: quos imbres montium rigant,  et non habentes velamen, amplexantur lapides.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִזֶּ֣רֶם הָרִ֣ים יִרְטָ֑בוּ וּֽמִבְּלִ֥י מַ֝חְסֶ֗ה חִבְּקוּ־צֽוּר׃
verses.king_james: They are wet with the showers of the mountains, and embrace the rock for want of a shelter.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vim fecerunt deprædantes pupillos,  et vulgum pauperem spoliaverunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִ֭גְזְלוּ מִשֹּׁ֣ד יָת֑וֹם וְֽעַל־עָנִ֥י יַחְבֹּֽלוּ׃
verses.king_james: They pluck the fatherless from the breast, and take a pledge of the poor.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nudis et incedentibus absque vestitu,  et esurientibus tulerunt spicas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָר֣וֹם הִ֭לְּכוּ בְּלִ֣י לְב֑וּשׁ וּ֝רְעֵבִ֗ים נָ֣שְׂאוּ עֹֽמֶר׃
verses.king_james: They cause him to go naked without clothing, and they take away the sheaf from the hungry;

verses.latin_vulgate: Inter acervos eorum meridiati sunt,  qui calcatis torcularibus sitiunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בֵּין־שׁוּרֹתָ֥ם יַצְהִ֑ירוּ יְקָבִ֥ים דָּ֝רְכ֗וּ וַיִּצְמָֽאוּ׃
verses.king_james: Which make oil within their walls, and tread their winepresses, and suffer thirst.

verses.latin_vulgate: De civitatibus fecerunt viros gemere,  et anima vulneratorum clamavit :  et Deus inultum abire non patitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵ֘עִ֤יר מְתִ֨ים ׀ יִנְאָ֗קוּ וְנֶֽפֶשׁ־חֲלָלִ֥ים תְּשַׁוֵּ֑עַ וֶ֝אֱל֗וֹהַּ לֹֽא־יָשִׂ֥ים תִּפְלָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Men groan from out of the city, and the soul of the wounded crieth out: yet God layeth not folly to them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipsi fuerunt rebelles lumini :  nescierunt vias ejus,  nec reversi sunt per semitas ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵ֤מָּה ׀ הָיוּ֮ בְּֽמֹרְדֵ֫י־א֥וֹר לֹא־הִכִּ֥ירוּ דְרָכָ֑יו וְלֹ֥א יָ֝שְׁב֗וּ בִּנְתִיבֹתָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: They are of those that rebel against the light; they know not the ways thereof, nor abide in the paths thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mane primo consurgit homicida ;  interficit egenum et pauperem :  per noctem vero erit quasi fur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָא֡וֹר יָ֘ק֤וּם רוֹצֵ֗חַ יִֽקְטׇל־עָנִ֥י וְאֶבְי֑וֹן וּ֝בַלַּ֗יְלָה יְהִ֣י כַגַּנָּֽב׃
verses.king_james: The murderer rising with the light killeth the poor and needy, and in the night is as a thief.

verses.latin_vulgate: Oculus adulteri observat caliginem,  dicens : Non me videbit oculus :  et operiet vultum suum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעֵ֤ין נֹאֵ֨ף ׀ שָׁ֤מְרָֽה־נֶ֣שֶׁף לֵ֭אמֹר לֹא־תְשׁוּרֵ֣נִי עָ֑יִן וְסֵ֖תֶר פָּנִ֣ים יָשִֽׂים׃
verses.king_james: The eye also of the adulterer waiteth for the twilight, saying, No eye shall see me: and disguiseth his face.

verses.latin_vulgate: Perfodit in tenebris domos,  sicut in die condixerant sibi,  et ignoraverunt lucem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חָתַ֥ר בַּחֹ֗שֶׁךְ בָּ֫תִּ֥ים יוֹמָ֥ם חִתְּמוּ־לָ֗מוֹ לֹא־יָ֥דְעוּ אֽוֹר׃
verses.king_james: In the dark they dig through houses, which they had marked for themselves in the daytime: they know not the light.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si subito apparuerit aurora, arbitrantur umbram mortis :  et sic in tenebris quasi in luce ambulant.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י יַחְדָּ֨ו ׀ בֹּ֣קֶר לָ֣מוֹ צַלְמָ֑וֶת כִּֽי־יַ֝כִּ֗יר בַּלְה֥וֹת צַלְמָֽוֶת׃
verses.king_james: For the morning is to them even as the shadow of death: if one know them, they are in the terrors of the shadow of death.

verses.latin_vulgate: Levis est super faciem aquæ :  maledicta sit pars ejus in terra,  nec ambulet per viam vinearum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קַל־ה֤וּא ׀ עַל־פְּנֵי־מַ֗יִם תְּקֻלַּ֣ל חֶלְקָתָ֣ם בָּאָ֑רֶץ לֹא־יִ֝פְנֶ֗ה דֶּ֣רֶךְ כְּרָמִֽים׃
verses.king_james: He is swift as the waters; their portion is cursed in the earth: he beholdeth not the way of the vineyards.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ad nimium calorem transeat ab aquis nivium,  et usque ad inferos peccatum illius.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: צִיָּ֤ה גַם־חֹ֗ם יִגְזְל֥וּ מֵֽימֵי־שֶׁ֗לֶג שְׁא֣וֹל חָטָֽאוּ׃
verses.king_james: Drought and heat consume the snow waters: so doth the grave those which have sinned.

verses.latin_vulgate: Obliviscatur ejus misericordia ; dulcedo illius vermes :  non sit in recordatione,  sed conteratur quasi lignum infructuosum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִשְׁכָּ֘חֵ֤הוּ רֶ֨חֶם ׀ מְתָ֘ק֤וֹ רִמָּ֗ה ע֥וֹד לֹֽא־יִזָּכֵ֑ר וַתִּשָּׁבֵ֖ר כָּעֵ֣ץ עַוְלָֽה׃
verses.king_james: The womb shall forget him; the worm shall feed sweetly on him; he shall be no more remembered; and wickedness shall be broken as a tree.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pavit enim sterilem quæ non parit,  et viduæ bene non fecit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רֹעֶ֣ה עֲ֭קָרָה לֹ֣א תֵלֵ֑ד וְ֝אַלְמָנָ֗ה לֹ֣א יְיֵטִֽיב׃
verses.king_james: He evil entreateth the barren that beareth not: and doeth not good to the widow.

verses.latin_vulgate: Detraxit fortes in fortitudine sua,  et cum steterit, non credet vitæ suæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמָשַׁ֣ךְ אַבִּירִ֣ים בְּכֹח֑וֹ יָ֝ק֗וּם וְֽלֹא־יַאֲמִ֥ין בַּחַיִּֽין׃
verses.king_james: He draweth also the mighty with his power: he riseth up, and no man is sure of life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dedit ei Deus locum pœnitentiæ,  et ille abutitur eo in superbiam :  oculi autem ejus sunt in viis illius.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִתֶּן־ל֣וֹ לָ֭בֶטַח וְיִשָּׁעֵ֑ן וְ֝עֵינֵ֗יהוּ עַל־דַּרְכֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: Though it be given him to be in safety, whereon he resteth; yet his eyes are upon their ways.

verses.latin_vulgate: Elevati sunt ad modicum, et non subsistent :  et humiliabuntur sicut omnia, et auferentur,  et sicut summitates spicarum conterentur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ר֤וֹמּוּ מְּעַ֨ט ׀ וְֽאֵינֶ֗נּוּ וְֽהֻמְּכ֗וּ כַּכֹּ֥ל יִקָּפְצ֑וּן וּכְרֹ֖אשׁ שִׁבֹּ֣לֶת יִמָּֽלוּ׃
verses.king_james: They are exalted for a little while, but are gone and brought low; they are taken out of the way as all other, and cut off as the tops of the ears of corn.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod si non est ita, quis me potest arguere esse mentitum,  et ponere ante Deum verba mea ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִם־לֹ֣א אֵ֭פוֹ מִ֣י יַכְזִיבֵ֑נִי וְיָשֵׂ֥ם לְ֝אַ֗ל מִלָּתִֽי׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: And if it be not so now, who will make me a liar, and make my speech nothing worth?
